text_original:
A legally blind Ormond Beach man was attempting to wash dishes Tuesday evening when his father yelled and spit at him for not cleaning them right because of his blindness, an official.

Oscar Martinez is legally blind and has no peripheral vision, according to Ormond Beach Police Chief Gary Ratliff.

"My father does a lot of things that are normal for him," Ratliff said. "It's just part of who he is."

The incident occurred about 6:30 p.m. Tuesday evening at the family's home on Southwest 15th Street.

Martinez had just finished washing a plate when his father, Oscar Martinez Sr., asked him to put it away, Ratliff said.

When Oscar Martinez refused to do so, his father then began yelling at him, Ratliff said.

"He spit in my face and said, 'You're stupid. You don't know anything,'" Ratliff said.

Police arrested Oscar Martinez Jr. after booking him into the Volusia County Branch Jail. He was charged with battery on a person who cannot see, police said.

Oscar Martinez Sr. was charged with disorderly conduct, Ratliff said.

"

text_original:
We learned last week that the radio show Brian Ward and I host on AM 1280 the Patriot is coming to an end.

The station had a letter to staff members sent out last Friday informing them of Ward’s decision to leave the show, which has been around since 1986.

Ward told the station he was leaving because he wants to spend more time with his family.

I spoke with Ward earlier this week and he said the decision to leave the show was personal.

“I need to be with my family,” Ward said. “In fact, I’m going to step back from my radio career and take a few months off to really make sure we have that time together.”

Ward said he’d like to take a role in the community.

“I think radio is great for people to be able to speak out on issues of public interest and to be able to have the ability to put their voice out there and their opinions out on the airwaves,” Ward said, “and I think we are going to miss it and we are going to miss the radio show.”

It’s a sad reality that it’s all over for the radio program.

text_original:
NEW DELHI: Over fifteen years after the Concorde — the world’s first and only supersonic aircraft to be used commercially — retired, US aerospace major Boeing has announced it is working on.

According to the company, it is developing a supersonic aircraft that will take off and land like a traditional jetliner, but travel at about four times the speed of sound.

The company, which also makes the 747 jumbo jet, hopes to start working on the new aircraft in the next decade, with the ultimate goal being to deliver the “world’s most efficient long-range passenger aircraft”.

“We are very excited to announce that we have partnered with NASA and our commercial partners to try to take this technology to the next level, through the development of a new supersonic aircraft and a supersonic business jet,” said Jim McNerney, vice-president of research and development at Boeing.

“Each of these technologies can help bridge the gap between where we are today and where we want to go,” he told PTI.

The company’s initiative comes at a time when the US is trying to slash its dependence on foreign suppliers for defence and space equipment by looking to develop as many domestic industries as possible.

text_original:
BOSTON (AP) — An Amtrak employee suffered an apparent heart attack and later died after a train struck a piece of construction equipment near where he was working early Thursday morning, officials.

The 47-year-old man was riding in the train when it crashed into the construction equipment, which was parked on the tracks. He was rushed to a hospital but died of his injuries.

Amtrak spokesman Marc Magliari said the employee had been working for the company for about four years. He was identified as Matt Browning, who worked in the company’s eastern service area.

The man’s name was not immediately released.

Amtrak said it was temporarily suspending its service between Boston and New York City as a precaution.

“Amtrak express trains from Boston to New York City are temporarily suspended due to a collision with construction equipment in the area of Framingham, Mass.,” Magliari said. “Service will resume at 2:30 a.m. local time and nonstop service between Boston and New York City is expected to be restored by 6 a.m.”

– Weird Al Yankovic and the Voices of Madness

“Punching bag!”

“There is no God.”

– Weird Al Yankovic and the Voices of Madness

text_original:
On Wednesday 2nd May the Gaza Center for Political and Development Studies staged a ceremony to award a group of writers and activists addressing prisoners’ issues. President Mahmoud Abbas selected the winners from a list of applicants.

The ceremony was attended by High Representative of the European Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy Federica Mogherini, as well as representatives of the Palestinian Prisoner Society [PPS], UNRWA, the Palestinian Authority and the Ministry of Labor.

The honorees were awarded with a commemorative plaque and a cheque for 200,000 shekels [USD 47,000]. The recipients of the prize included writer and human rights activist Rida al-Najjar, poet Mohamed Khaled, activist Ahmed Zaki, journalist Ali al-Sarraj, and student Nidal al-Mashali.

During the ceremony the Honorees also delivered speeches which included addressing the issues of prisoners’ issues, and calling for their immediate release.

The PPS has been aware of the fact that prisoners are routinely tortured and abused in Israeli prisons at the Condor military prison and the Ma’ale Adumim military prison.

text_original:
France was stepping up efforts on Monday to persuade world powers to impose a no-fly zone over Libya, as Muammar Gaddafi’s troops battled rebel fighters for control of the strategic oil town.

The Mediterranean country’s parliament overwhelmingly backed a resolution to back France in the bombing campaign, which is being led by French forces, and it could be put to a referendum later this year.

The vote went against rebel demands for an immediate halt to air strikes, and many Libyans see the vote as a ploy to buy time for the UN-backed government in Tripoli to take control of the capital and negotiate with Gaddafi.

Libya has been mired in chaos since the NATO-backed uprising that toppled Gaddafi four years ago and prompted a strongman ruler to stoke the flames of sectarian warfare, killing hundreds of people.

Protesters clash with tear gas during clashes between armed groups loyal to Colonel Gaddafi in Benghazi, Libya (Mohamed El Jayoumi/AP)

Gaddafi has refused to step down, and his forces are still fighting rebels across the country with the help of mercenaries from Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Chad.

A no-fly zone would not necessarily be a long-term solution, as it would likely only be enforced in areas where it was safe for planes to fly.

EXCLUSIVE: Kodi Smit-McPhee has scored a lead role in 20th Century Fox‘s Dawn Of The Planet Of The Apes.

The film will be directed from a script by Matt Reeves of the upcoming Dawn Of The Planet Of The Apes prequel Dark Dawn. It is set to premiere on June 21, 2018.

Smit-McPhee is set as Caesar. The character is described as an “appalled and furious gorilla.” He is described as the leader of a group of apes who are beginning to question their own existence.

Smit-McPhee joins a cast led by Andy Serkis and Ian McKellen who are set to star as Caesar and Rafe Spall. Serkis is also attached as Benicio del Toro’s father.

Smit-McPhee is repped by the WME, Brillstein Entertainment Partners and Gersh.

Steve Spurrier is producing along with Matt Reeves, David Heyman and John Davis of Atomic Monster.

Reeves wrote the script for Dark Dawn based on the novel by John Boyega.

text_original:
Max Verstappen has fired back at his critics, urging them to judge Sebastian Vettel as harshly as they did him after the German��s first lap antics in the French Grand Prix on.

The Dutchman said he was amazed that people in the media would try to compare the two drivers, saying he is proud and happy that Vettel is still racing for Ferrari.

"I don't understand why they are making such a big deal of it," Verstappen told reporters after Sunday's race. "He's got a lot of miles under his belt and he's been very lucky with the conditions."

Verstappen finished second behind Lewis Hamilton in the French Grand Prix, and impressed on his debut in Formula One.

The 21-year-old has built up a huge following in a short time on the F1 circuit with a catchy and engaging demeanour that has endeared him to fans worldwide.

But he has also been criticised by some for his driving style, and for not showcasing enough of his talent.

That was highlighted by Vettel on Sunday, who took aim at Verstappen's overtaking manoeuvres, calling them "unprofessional", while suggesting he had not done enough to earn the title of best driver in F1.

text_original:
Colorado legislators have narrowly voted in a bill that would establish state-run financial services for marijuana sellers, in an effort to ease the cash-only burden afflicting commercial weed distributors.

The Senate voted 27-19 Monday to pass House Bill 1212, providing a business model for dispensaries and growers to use the state’s existing online banking system to offer services to consumers. The bill now heads back to the House for further review.

“Without a doubt, there are some small businesses who are pretty much going to be shut out of the banking system because they don’t have enough money to get the money off their balance sheet,” said Sen. John Cooke, D-Fort Collins, chairman of the Senate Finance Committee. “This will give them the chance to do so.”

In addition to the online banking option, the bill also establishes a new fee for marijuana retailers — $10 for the first $500 worth of cannabis sold, with the rate decreasing by 50 percent for each additional $1,000 worth.

The package passed on Monday came after a lengthy discussion in both chambers of the General Assembly over several days this past spring.

The bill now heads back to the House for further review . “ Without a doubt , there are some small businesses who are pretty much going to be shut out of the banking system because they do n’t have enough money to get the money off their balance sheet , ” said Sen. John Cooke , D - Fort Collins , chairman of the Senate Finance Committee . “ This will give them the chance to do so . ” In addition to the online banking option , the bill also establishes a new fee for marijuana retailers — $ 10 for the first $ 500 worth of cannabis sold , with the rate decreasing by 50 percent for each additional $ 1,000 worth . After a lengthy discussion in both chambers of the General Assembly over several days this past spring, the package passed on Monday.

TEN couples in Cagayan de Oro City finally tied the knot in front of the altar last Saturday, March 23, during the mass wedding rites held at St. Augustine Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception. The couples are from different barangays in the city and are all students of the Cagayan de Oro University.

The wedding was officiated by Archbishop Chito Jose de Guzman of Cagayan de Oro City.

“I would like to congratulate all of them for their patience, perseverance and perseverance to make this happen,” Archbishop Chito said.

ADVERTISEMENT

“This is a very important day because it reflects a large part of our country’s faith. Let us not forget that in the Philippines, it is the Christian faith that guides us in every aspect of life,” he added.

The cakes were made from the red food dye made from the blood of St. Anthony of Padua.

The bride, Lourdes Geanayan, 32, hails from Barangay Na-a, Cagayan de Oro City. She is the granddaughter of a former mayor of the city.

Buckeye Valley’s Zach Kreft completed the trifecta at Tuesday and Thursday’s MOAC Championships, winning individual titles in the 800, 1,600 and 3,200.

A second-place finish in the 3,200 was enough to earn him a berth to the NCAA Division III Indoor Championships in North Carolina.

The Indians sent one to nationals, too.

Cary Pugh will begin his college career with a trip to Boston College as he qualified for the Big Dance in the 800 meters.

Pugh will be joined by teammates Mason Parkerson (400) and Dylan Watt (800), as well as Bryan Silverman (1,600) and Ben Rupp (3,200).

Pugh and Parkerson, both recent Buckeye Valley graduates, have been working on their endurance this season, and Pugh said it has helped prepare them for the big time.

“I had no idea what to expect from Boston College,” Pugh said. “It’s a tough place to go to, but I am ready.”

Watt will also be attending Boston College, along with Pugh and Silverman.

text_original:
Bangalore: In the U.S., there are prizes for being “the first to arrive” at a movie premiere, and there are awards for being the first to get into a movie on opening day. In India, the two competitions have become part of the fabric of Indian culture.

Celebrities have competed to be the first in the city — and the movie industry — to see a certain film. The first to discover a new film, for example, is often a businessperson who has betting interests on which film will do best.

But in Bangalore, both those rivalries can come to an end when the film has been released, and there is no one else to see it.

The city has 9,000 movie screens, but the average run time of any film is about 1 minute, according to Bollywood’s top star Amitabh Bachchan.

The Indian government’s minister of state for Information Technology and Telecommunication, Suresh Prabhu, visited Bangalore to make the case to the United States that the country needs to protect its film industry from foreign competitors.

The first to discover a new film , for example , is often a businessperson who has betting interests on which film will do best . But in Bangalore , both those rivalries can come to an end when the film has been released , and there is no one else to see it . The city has 9,000 movie screens , but the average run time of any film is about 1 minute , according to Bollywood ’s top star Amitabh Bachchan . The Indian government ’s minister of state for Information Technology and Telecommunication , Suresh Prabhu , visited Bangalore to make the case to the United States that the country needs to protect its film industry from foreign competitors .

text_original:
On Nov. 2, when “Bohemian Rhapsody” — the biopic of Freddie Mercury, lead singer of British rock group Queen — debuts on U.S. screens, it will mark latest in a long string.

In the past year alone, there were two films about former President Barack Obama, one about Robert Kennedy and another about the late actress Carrie Fisher, whose mother Debbie Reynolds died in December.

The latter, “Star Wars: The Last Jedi,” opened last month with rave reviews but has become a major box-office disappointment.

In the past year, there have been five biopics and three films about the death of Kennedy.

The most successful of these is “King Kong,” which grossed $366 million worldwide and was nominated for four Academy Awards.

A look at some of the biggest film releases in the past year:

___

BERLIN — The director of the new film about the murder of Berlin’s famed center-to-east metro line, “Darkest Hour,” says he couldn’t have made the movie without paying tribute to the late 10th century Mongol warrior Genghis Khan and his wife, Ula, who were instrumental in establishing the dynasty.

Is Agunah Tamar Epstein's Remarriage Legal Without Orthodox Divorce?
The actress, who has been married three times before, is now in a new relationship with a Russian-born businessman — and she's got a question for her ex-husband.
Wonderful news! That’s right! The new Mrs. Epstein is back! With a new man, no less. And Agunah Tamar Epstein and her new beau — the aforementioned businessman — are now officially divorced from ex-husband Adam Epstein.
But what does that mean for Agunah and her ex’s new marriage? Is it legal?
Well, there’s some confusion about this whole situation.
A spokesperson for the actress recently told E! News that, “Agunah and Adam have officially separated at this time. They remain good friends.”
It’s unclear if Agunah and Adam are currently living together, but we do know that they had a very public split back in March 2019.
In an interview with Us Weekly at the time, Adam explained why he decided to split from Agunah: “I think it's because we're so much better off as individuals than we were together as a couple,” he said.

text_original:
The Maryland teenager who terrorized his high school used his father's handgun to shoot a girl he'd just broken up with and another classmate, investigators said Wednesday.

None of the victims, who were shot in the shoulder after they tried to stop the teen from pulling the trigger, is expected to survive.

The shooter, 16-year-old Abdul Razak Ali Artan, was "shocked by the violence" at Charles County High School, Charles County Sheriff Mike Rutherford said.

"He felt like this was an opportunity to kill someone," Rutherford said.

Artan was taken into custody Tuesday at a home in Antietam, Md., about 20 miles from the school where the shooting happened. He had been living at his father's home, where the other victims were taken when Baltimore County police arrested him.

The gunman is not believed to have had any contact with law enforcement during a two-month period before the shooting. He was reported as a runaway on May 28, and was arrested May 31 after being spotted by a deputy who stopped him on Interstate 95 near Baltimore.

Police believe Artan's father bought the handgun used in the shooting from another person.

text_original:
Labyrinths of Memory (Matahat El-Zakirah) play will be performed several times over three days at Osiris art space, the same location that hosted the play in December 2015.

The performance features the three members of Matahat El-Zakirah, Mohammed Al-Barash, Behrouz Shamsi and Amy Hoffman, as well as a Q&A with them afterwards.

“We are very excited to present this work again with the support of the artist-run space Osiris,” says Al-Barash. “Osiris has been an important part of our art practice since its inception and we’re looking forward to coming back there. The space works well for us because it represents a hub for creative practice in the Muslim community, and is a place where we can connect with people and build a community of shared experiences,” he adds.

“Osiris is excited to have Matahat El-Zakirah again at the centre of our community and to continue nurturing their engagement with the arts,” says Farshad Dehghani, director of Osiris.

“This space has been the home of Matahat El-Zakirah for many years and continues to develop into a vibrant creative hub for the Muslim community,” he concludes.

text_original:
The design, which features a 452-bedroom hotel plus apartments, was approved by the authority last week but must now win a green-light from the London mayor’s office.

Set for completion in the spring of 2018, the £120m project will join the Kentish Town’s rapidly growing raft of luxury hotels and apartments, including the Shard, Park Lane and the new Millennium Point.

The site, on the corner of Park Road and Hackney Road, will be joined by a £32m residential development by developers Rydon Group, which also owns the nearby Park Lane Hotel.

Built over a period of nine years, the hotel will be constructed on the site of the former Howard Johnson’s pub, with its own entrance off Hackney Road.

Other buildings on the plot, which is currently fenced off, include an office block and a small retail shop.

The design is by architects Heatherwick Studio and was submitted in response to a planning application from the developer for the development.

Councillors sitting on Harrow Council’s planning committee voted unanimously to approve the scheme last week.

George Smith, chairman of the planning committee, said: “This is a major project that has been well thought out, carefully considered and carefully designed.

text_original:
Stamford's Old Town Hall Redevelopment Agency this week chose Nov. 13, 2015, as the date for a groundbreaking ceremony for the 20,000-square-foot addition to the Old Town Hall building.

The building, with a $14.1 million price tag, will be located on the site of a former police station at the corner of Main and Cedar Streets in Stamford's Old Town neighborhood.

"I am very excited about this project, and I am very glad that the old police station has been included," said Stamford Mayor Mark Boughton. "In the future, I hope we can have a police and fire department station on this site."

Stamford City Council members unanimously approved the redevelopment agency's purchase of the former police station site in early October.

The city is expected to invest about $6.4 million in the project.

Construction of the police station is expected to begin in January 2016, and the building is expected to open in July 2017.

Property owner Town of Stamford Development Corporation has estimated construction costs at more than $12 million, according to the mayor's office.

text_original:
Apple (NSDQ: AAPL) today announced details for its upcoming five-day Worldwide Developers Conference starting June 9. The company will hold a special event called WWDC21, where it will discuss updates to its operating systems and new hardware products.

Apple is hosting the event in San Jose, California, and will broadcast it live on its website. The company says that WWDC21 will include an update to its iOS, macOS, watchOS, tvOS, and watchOS apps and software. There’s also a chance that Apple will announce new Macs and additional Apple Watch versions of its smartwatches.

The company also plans to share updates to its TV app, as well as its iPhone app. Apple may also introduce new iPad models at WWDC.

Apple’s announcement comes less than a week after the company announced that it is dropping the price of its entry-level iPhone 12 line by $100.

The new iPhones include a 5.4-inch screen, a new A14 Bionic chip, and new cameras. The base model includes a 6.1-inch display with a resolution of 1080×2340 pixels, while the most powerful version has a larger 7.5-inch display with a resolution of 2160×1080 pixels.

A 6.1 - inch display with a resolution of 1080×2340 pixels is included in the base model, while a larger 7.5 - inch display with a resolution of 2160×1080 pixels has the most powerful version.

Today, Apple ( NSDQ : AAPL ) announced details for its upcoming Worldwide Developers Conference starting June 9, which will last five days. Called WWDC21, a special event will be held by the company, where updates to its operating systems and new hardware products will be discussed. The event is being hosted by Apple in San Jose, California, and it will be broadcast live on its website. The company says that WWDC21 will include an update to its iOS , macOS , watchOS , tvOS , and watchOS apps and software . There’s also a chance that new Macs and additional versions of Apple Watch smartwatches will be announced by Apple. Updates to its TV app, as well as its iPhone app, the company also plans to share. At WWDC, new iPad models may also be introduced by Apple. The senior lost an opportunity to go for two after the Ducks lost the ball on their first possession of the game. Instead, Thomas raced down the sideline and ran into the end zone for the 76-yard score against Arizona State, now ranked No. 16 in the BCS poll, to open the 2007 season.

Thomas had a chance to go for two on the next play, but he was tackled at the Wildcats� 40 yard line. He started looking around for a flat spot, but he couldn�t find one that would give him the same kind of distance as the previous one.

It�s not unusual for the 6-foot, 218-pound Thomas to have trouble finding a hole, but it�s been difficult to run the ball this season. He has only played in six games, including the last two games, because of knee injuries.

text_original:
Michelle Morris was surprised when her mom handed her the May 24th issue of Sports Illustrated and she saw her name and face staring back at her with a small write-up.

"I didn't know who it was," Michelle said. "I researched it, and I was like, 'Oh my gosh, I am in Sports Illustrated!"

It was a surprise when she found out who the anonymous writer was.

"It was definitely a shocker that someone would do that to me," said Morris, who is currently competing in the 2010 U.S. Open Golf Championship at the Manhattan Country Club.

Morris' story will be featured in the May 24th edition of Sports Illustrated, but Sports Illustrated isn't just going to put a photo of Morris on the cover.

The magazine plans to include her story in its print edition but will also get the younger Morris to write an article.

"I can't wait to see what she does," said Sports Illustrated's editor in chief, Gary McNamara. "It's a great opportunity for her to expand her sports writing resume while still in high school."

Sports Illustrated didn't give Morris a deadline for the article, but she hopes to have it ready for the May 24th print edition.

text_original:
Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has doled out sops and freebies to dalits, backward classes, minorities and economically weaker sections, considered the Congress’ vote bank, in the 2014-15 budget ahead of the crucial Lok.

The Chief Minister has announced a slew of measures in his second budget speech for the 2014-15 fiscal, which ends on March 31.

He has promised free LPG connections to Dalits (2.5 lakh), backward classes (5 lakh) and economically weaker sections (10 lakh), besides providing free electricity connections to all households.

The Chief Minister has also promised free education up to Class X to all children belonging to Dalits, backward classes and economically weaker sections.

Similarly, he has promised free medical treatment to Dalit women who fall in the 12th and 15th standard.

The Chief Minister has also promised free education and other benefits for widows and widowers of Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs).

The government is taking steps to provide free education and other benefits to Dalit girls in government schools.

The government has also given promises to provide free water connections to Dalits and other backward classes.

Similarly , he has promised free medical treatment to Dalit women who fall in the 12th and 15th standard . The Chief Minister has also promised free education and other benefits for widows and widowers of Scheduled Castes ( SCs ) and Scheduled Tribes ( STs ) . The government is taking steps to provide free education and other benefits to Dalit girls in government schools . The government has also given promises to provide free water connections to Dalits and other backward classes .

text_original:
The indictments against a Pineville brother and sister in a triple murder case provide a few more details in the October case in which the three bodies were found in two spots.

The brothers, who are both 18 years old, have been charged with capital murder in the deaths of Catherine Linnehan, 18, and Leontes "Lenny" Linnehan, 19, and Andrew Linnehan, 20, allegedly tied up and tortured for over a week before being killed.

Both brothers were arrested at the family's home on Oct. 13, after they were caught on an emergency beacon owned by Lenny Linnehan.

Andrew Linnehan was bound and gagged before he was strangled with his own shirt, according to court documents. The Linneshan girls were bound and gagged and beaten with sticks until they were dead.

A fourth individual, a woman, was found dead in a barn alongside the bodies, police said.

One of the defendants, who is also named Andrew, is from Pineville, but lived elsewhere prior to the murders. His father, Joseph, owns a home on La. 182 south of Pineville.

text_original:
It is a nightmare scenario hospitals must be prepared for, although they hope those preparations will never be used.

The New South Wales government last week said it would make emergency plans to deal with a large influx of patients at the state's hospitals should coronavirus cases surge.

But as NSW Health says it is too early to consider such plans, a group of doctors and nurses are calling on the state and federal governments to act urgently to ensure there is enough hospital staff and equipment.

Overnight, the number of confirmed cases in Australia has jumped from two to 21.

"There definitely will be people who need to be taken to hospital," Dr Diana Allen told 7NEWS.com.au.

"We don't know how many, but there will certainly be people who need to be taken to hospital."

NSW Health says it's too early to think about a scenario where the state's hospitals are overwhelmed by patients with coronavirus. ( Supplied )

Dr Allen is one of the senior medical officers on the coronavirus response team within Sydney's Eastern Suburbs Hospital, which is currently dealing with an outbreak of four patients.

The number of confirmed cases in Australia has jumped from two to 21 overnight. "Dr. Diana Allen told 7NEWS.com.au, 'There will certainly be people who need to be taken to hospital. We don't know how many, but there definitely will be people who need to be taken to hospital.'" " It 's too early to think about a scenario where the state 's hospitals are overwhelmed by patients with coronavirus, NSW Health says." ( Supplied ) Dr Allen is one of the senior medical officers on the coronavirus response team within Sydney 's Eastern Suburbs Hospital , which is currently dealing with an outbreak of four patients .

text_original:
Claude Isaacs and Eric Roubbi, two French nationals accused of killing Lee Zeitouni in a hit-and-run case in Tel Aviv last September said Thursday that a trial held in Israel would be.

Zeitouni was run over by his rental car in the city center and died from his injuries. The driver of the car fled the scene and never faced any charges.

The two men were arrested on suspicion of kidnapping and murder in France in late July, but the case has been held up due to the lack of a country willing to extradite them to Israel.

In a statement released Thursday, the two convicted murderers said they had decided to accept an offer offered by Israeli Justice Minister Tzipi Livni’s office to close the case against them.

“We are convinced that this is the best way for us to open a new chapter in our lives,” the two men said.

Get The Times of Israel's Daily Edition by email and never miss our top stories Free Sign Up

They also said they would pay compensation to Zeitouni’s family.

Zeitouni’s family has been left devastated by the killing.

text_original:
The final group of Irish peacekeepers to serve in Chad arrived back in Dublin this afternoon.

President Michael D Higgins met with the group after they arrived at Dublin Airport.

The group of 17 men and three women, aged between 18 and 20, are from Ireland’s 4th Battalion The Royal Irish Regiment.

They have been deployed with the UN Mission in Chad since October last year, and will now return to their base in N’Djamena, in the West African nation.

Ireland’s deployment to Chad is part of a growing number of peacekeeping missions that Ireland is involved in.

Last week saw the arrival of more Irish troops to Mali, where they are currently supporting efforts to protect civilians after an Islamist rebel attack on the Malian army.

Earlier this year, Prime Minister Enda Kenny visited Mali, the Central African Republic and Chad to discuss ways in which Ireland could support its neighbours as they struggle to combat armed groups.

Mr Higgins said he was “delighted” to welcome the group back to Ireland.

text_original:
SACRAMENTO — Returning to his alma mater, C.J. Beathard expects this season to be special for Oakland Raiders quarterback Derek Carr.

“I’m very excited,” said Beathard, who played at the University of Nevada-Reno from 2001-03. “It’s cool to come back and play here and show the Raider Nation what we want to do, what we love to do, and what we can do. I think it’s cool that we’re coming off a good season last year and we’ve added to it this offseason.”

The Raiders (1-0) will open the season against the Los Angeles Chargers on Sept. 10 at the Coliseum.

Last year, the Raiders were 2-14 under first-year coach Jack Del Rio, but they rode a dominant defense and a young, dynamic passing attack to their first division title since 2002.

Now, they’re replacing starting quarterback Derek Carr with Beathard, who helped lead the Broncos to a 12-4 record in 2012 and the AFC West title.

We have an entire generation of folks who have no idea how to change the own oil in the car.
So you're telling me you changed your own oil in your grandma's car?
My dad used to change my moms car for her. He could've been a mechanic and paid someone else to do it, but he was the only person who knew how to change oil.
Well I suppose that makes sense...   In any case that's awesome! That's the best kind of story. Happy Mother's Day to both of you!
He's not a bad guy, just a bit stubborn. Happy Mother's Day to both of you too!
Now you're just making this hard for me!
I don't even know why I put that in the first place, I'm just adding to your confusion.
You're being nice, I appreciate it!
Oh, no worries, I shouldn't have put that in the first place. Thanks for all the encouragement.
You're welcome!   I hope you have a great day!
Well, it didn’t turn out as well as my initial plan.

text_original:
The United States Defence Secretary, Donald Rumsfeld, says that the US cannot afford to wait for conclusive proof of Saddam Hussein's weapons programs before it attacks Iraq.

"We don't want to wait," he said of the current conflict in Iraq, which is being fought mainly by US forces. "It wouldn't be good for us, it wouldn't be good for the world and it would be bad for our ally, the United Arab Emirates."

Mr Rumsfeld was speaking at the launch of a new report on the war by the Pentagon's inspector-general, James E Stewart. The report was released after a bitterly fought battle between the US and Iraqi governments over information about the US campaign.

The report came seven months after the Pentagon suspended its investigation into allegations that Saddam Hussein had been trying to buy uranium from Niger.

The suspension was lifted in June last year when the US intelligence agencies agreed to a request by the Iraqi government to continue the investigation.

The investigation was held up by a dispute over how closely the US military should cooperate with the Iraqi government.

The US wants to obtain a full accounting of the bases where US forces have been operating and the arms they are equipped with, while the Iraqi government wants to know how many weapons have been found and destroyed during the war.

text_original:
The long wait for Tesla's new Model 3 is almost over. The company just announced that it will be delivering the Model 3 to customers in Houston and Austin, Texas, as of today.

The news comes just days after Tesla and its partners delivered the first batch of the Model 3 to an employee's home in Hawthorne, California.

Tesla has been playing a bit of catch up moving forward. On June 7th, it was revealed that Tesla's large order book for the Model 3 had reached 1,000 cars for the first time.

That was good news for Tesla, but also bad news for the company's workers.

We've known since late 2015 that Tesla was looking at building a factory outside of the United States.

CEO Elon Musk revealed that the company was considering building a factory outside of the United States during its fourth quarter earnings call.

Musk told investors that Tesla had "really started to think about the possibility of building a factory somewhere else. There are a lot of potential sites — some of which may be a little bit too risky."

CEO Elon Musk revealed that the company was considering building a factory outside of the United States during its fourth quarter earnings call . Musk told investors that Tesla had " really started to think about the possibility of building a factory somewhere else . There are a lot of potential sites — some of which may be a little bit too risky . "

The long wait for Tesla 's new Model 3 is almost over . As of today, the company just announced that it will be delivering the Model 3 to customers in Houston and Austin, Texas. Just days after Tesla and its partners delivered the first batch of the Model 3 to an employee's home in Hawthorne, California, the news comes. text_original:
Slowing economic growth in China is taking some of the glow off of one of the country’s greatest attractions for foreign businesses: consumers�� willingness to splurge on higher-priced goods as incomes rise.

Consumers in China’s second largest city, Shanghai, are increasingly choosing cheaper clothes and other items at a time when the economy is slowing.

The slowdown has been particularly bad this year in the city’s main shopping district, where sales of luxury items have fallen by about 10 percent over the past 12 months, according to candidates for a city leader on Thursday.

The city’s economy has cooled since early 2015, when the government began to cut taxes and other subsidies. That has led to an erosion in consumer confidence, which in turn has hurt the consumption of luxury items.

The slowdown has also affected the output of many overseas luxury brands, which rely heavily on Chinese consumers.

“As a whole, the demand for high-end brands has declined, and luxury consumption is starting to be affected,” said Li Ruigang, chief executive of the sales and marketing division of the Chinese fast-fashion retailer Zara.

“We are definitely experiencing lower overall business in China,” he said in a telephone interview from Zara’s Beijing headquarters.

text_original:
On June 17, hundreds of thousands of Dominicans of Haitian descent will be stateless. Some 600,000 of them are stateless. This is a shameful fact that the Dominican government has not recognized to date, despite the fact that it was established by legislation of the Dominican Congress in 1975 and ratified by the Dominican Parliament on January 7, 1996.

Unfortunately, this situation also affects the families of Haitian immigrants, since they are considered stateless and their rights as individuals and as families are not respected.

The Dominican government has taken measures to protect the rights of both Dominican citizens and Haitian immigrants, but it has not been enough. The Dominican government has already adopted legislation to remedy the situation, and the Haitian government has done so in the past years.

In 2009, the Dominican House of Representatives proposed a bill to make the Dominican nationality and the status of citizens of Haiti equal.

The proposal, however, was defeated. The Dominican Senate, however, presented a bill to make the Dominican nationality and the status of citizens of Haiti equal and replace the current Dominican nationality law.

The Dominican government has taken measures to protect the rights of both Dominican citizens and Haitian immigrants , but it has not been enough . The Dominican government has already adopted legislation to remedy the situation , and the Haitian government has done so in the past years . In 2009, a bill was proposed by the Dominican House of Representatives to make equal the status of citizens of Haiti and the Dominican nationality. The proposal , however , was defeated . The Dominican Senate , however , presented a bill to make the Dominican nationality and the status of citizens of Haiti equal and replace the current Dominican nationality law .

Sentenced: Maurice Hennegan, 25, was sentenced to 32 years to life in prision for murdering a man who was romantically involved with Hennegan’s estranged girlfriend.

During a hearing on Monday, Hennegan was handed the maximum sentence by Superior Court Judge John T. Hagan, which followed a guilty verdict last week that found Hennegan guilty of second-degree murder, second-degree manslaughter and discharging a firearm within city limits.

In addition to jail time, Hennegan will also have to pay $134,050 in restitution to the victim’s family, which will be determined at a later date.

Hughes, 27, was shot on the afternoon of Dec. 29, 2015 as he sat in his car at a gas station on the 7400 block of Bathurst St., near Seton Blvd.

“It’s the same thing over and over again,” Hughes’ sister, Nicole Hughes said last week, speaking outside court.

She thanked the victim’s loved ones for being strong, and she called both the shooter and her brother “evil people.”

The shooting happened as Hughes was getting out of his vehicle to get gas and leave the station.

BELLVILLE — Law enforcement officials are still trying to figure out why a man opened fire on another man late Sunday morning in a Delaware, Ohio rest area, severely injuring the man,.

On Monday, the Bellville Police Department released new information concerning the shooting.

Police originally said the victim was shot in the shoulder, but he has since been upgraded to able to speak, according to police. He told police that he had been at the rest area when he met up with the suspect, who got into an argument with another man and then left the area in a silver truck.

Police say the suspect is described as a white male, with brown hair and a beard. He was wearing a black baseball cap, a gray shirt, and gray shorts.

Anyone with information about the incident is asked to call the Bellville Police Department at 330-569-2550. Information can also be given at the police department website.

Comments (3)

The suspect is a black male with a beard. He was wearing a black baseball cap, a gray shirt, and gray shorts. He is also believed to have been driving a silver or grey pickup truck.

text_original:
Conventional wisdom will be put to the test in the 2020 Democratic primaries, according to Columbia, S.C., political scientist Tom Jensen.

Jensen said that while Bernie Sanders and Elizabeth Warren have been the two most prominent progressive candidates in the lead-up to the Iowa caucuses and New Hampshire primary, he expects former Vice President Joe Biden to gain steam as eight months of campaigning begin.

"I think both Warren and Sanders, though, are now viewed as weak," he said. "Both of them may say they're quite happy to run third, but I think it's going to be a nasty fight for second."

Biden has a long history of splitting the progressive vote at the presidential level, but he is not expected to have such problems this time around, given the relative strength of his campaign infrastructure and the relative performance of some of his rivals.

"The question for Biden is, 'Can he build a coalition? And can he build a base?'" noted Jensen. "That's a big question mark."

In an interview with Breitbart News Daily SiriusXM host Alex Marlow on Wednesday, Jensen said that Biden might be able to govern more effectively than many others because of a more moderate, pragmatic approach.

"

text_original:
Eli Sister (John C. Reilly) is a writer with dreams of becoming great. His work has made him a household name, but he's still not satisfied. So, he decides to take his talents to the big screen by writing a script based on a true story of the Super Bowl: "Mad Dog."

Nancy (Christina Hendricks) and her husband, Vince (John Goodman), have their share of accomplishments. They've been married for 16 years, have two beautiful children and live in a quaint, tree-lined neighborhood. But when a car accident leaves Nancy paralyzed from the waist down, Vince is left to care for their family and take care of Nancy's children, plus his own. That's a lot to do.

When Nancy's daughter, Samantha (Caitlin Carvey), tells Nancy about a man who can help them, she sets out with Vince to meet this man, Stan (Dan Stevens). Stan is an executive at a large corporation who suffers from a debilitating back injury, but he's willing to take all the risk that will help Nancy and Vince.

text_original:
Police and fire departments across the country are cracking down on officers for posting racist, violent memes and comments.

Memes are now being heavily reprimanded for making light of tragedies like mass shootings, or mocking police officers who have been killed.

In a statement from the police department in Lakeland, Florida, where a soldier was killed by a gunman at a local Walmart store, an officer was given a two-week suspension and a $250 fine as punishment.

“We find it unacceptable that a member of our police department, especially those who have sworn to serve and protect, would use a social media platform to discuss such tragic events,” said Chief Randy Smith.

The Lakeland Police Department also announced a similar suspension for a second officer over their own social media posts, this time including a meme that showed a pistol pointed at a man’s head.

A self-described “conservative conservative” in California has been arrested for threatening to kill a prominent black Democrat politician during a heated debate over police reforms in the state.

The suspect, Tyler Barriss, is a 25-year-old former student at the University of California, Davis, whom the university had kicked out of the school for disciplinary reasons.

text_original:
SAN FRANCISCO – A San Francisco UPS driver recovering from a gunshot wound said Monday in an interview that he does not understand why his colleague Jimmy Lam shot him and killed.

“I don’t know why Jimmy did that,” the man, who was shot nine times just outside his workplace at 8:15 a.m. last Thursday, told KTVU-TV. “I don’t know what made him do it.”

The person who shot Lam is still at large, authorities said.

The man, identified by police as 29-year-old Jimmy Lam, has been out of the hospital since the shooting, but he remains recovering.

Investigators said Lam worked for the company for four years. He remains hospitalized at San Francisco General Hospital.

Police said the gunman confronted Lam outside his workplace on the city’s Bayview District. Lam responded and shot him three times, one of which penetrated his rib cage. The gunman then fled in Lam’s car.

Authorities said Lam had no interaction with the gunman before the shooting.

Both men are Asian. Police have not released their names.

WALKERTOWN � Brenna Hicks hit five 3-pointers and finished with a game-high 18 points to lead visiting Alamance Christian School to a 42-30 victory against Gospel Light in the first round of.

WILMINGTON � Kynlee Boyd scored 11 of her 14 points in the first half, and then added seven more in the second to lead Burlington County to its fourth straight win, 49-28, over Manville on Tuesday night.

ALAMANCE, NC - The Green Wave took a 27-20 lead into halftime against Greenville at the ACHSI Girls Basketball State Tournament on Friday, and held on for a 50-44 victory against the Fighting Falcons.

text_original:
Dave Eggers' history as the motive force behind the experimental, expectations-challenging literary-goofery platform McSweeney's hangs heavily over his new book, What Is The What. Eggers is a master of the gimmick — he created the magazine in 2000 and it published its first issue in 2005 — but his obsession with igniting an audience for himself is still fresh.

In the book, Eggers describes a new kind of culture, one which is focused on maximizing participation rather than on interacting with others. As a result, "what is the what?" is the subject of the book's title.

"The What" is a central metaphor for the book, a way to explain how readers can participate in the process of experiencing what Eggers calls "the experience of the other." This is where the title comes from.

"What is the what?" is the first question Eggers asks readers: "Is there some magic that makes real life change into something you could call 'The What?'"

Throughout the book, Eggers presents a series of self-reflective exercises that attempt to answer this question: How do we make our world better? How do we make our world better for others?

text_original:
Super Bowl LIII wasn't the most exciting championship game, with the New England Patriots beating the Los Angeles Rams 13-3.

But it did deliver a champion's message: that no matter where you are, you can be close to winning the big one.

"I don't think anyone is better than any other team for me," Patriots defensive end Trey Flowers said after the game.

It was hard to argue with him; the Patriots won their sixth Super Bowl in 17 years, and they were the overwhelming favorite coming into the game.

And what's more, they did so without star quarterback Tom Brady, who was suspended for four games for his role in the 2018 Deflategate scandal.

That didn't stop them.

After losing to the Seattle Seahawks in 2000, the Baltimore Ravens won the next three Super Bowls without Brady, and the Kansas City Chiefs won the last two with Patrick Mahomes at quarterback.

Even if Brady is back at some point this season, his record still stands — he's the NFL's all-time leader in touchdown passes (541) and interceptions (64).

Even if Brady is back at some point this season , his record still stands — he 's the NFL 's all - time leader in touchdown passes ( 541 ) and interceptions ( 64 ) . "

text_original:
Encouraging good eating habits in children, sustainable diets and food as medicine will be among the topics discussed at a major event this weekend.

The annual forum hosted by the RSPCA and supported by the Irish Heart Foundation will bring together experts to discuss the potential link between diet and chronic diseases, including heart disease, cancer, diabetes and obesity.

While heart disease has been on the rise over the last few years, according to the latest figures it is the most preventable cause of death for both males and females in Ireland.

The number of men dying from cardiovascular disease in Ireland increased by 8%, to just under 4,000, last year, with women accounting for more than 3,300 deaths, according to RSPCA president Marie O’Connor.

“Heart disease is the number one killer of men and women in Ireland,” she said.

“It’s important to remember two things: first, heart disease is preventable; second, those who are overweight or obese have a much higher risk of suffering from heart disease.

“We know that there is a strong correlation between weight and how we feel about our own bodies.

text_original:
SALT LAKE CITY — Recent reports show that Utah's charter schools are improving, but are they doing enough to keep up with charter schools in other states?

It turns out the answer is no.

The Education Commission of Utah found the Utah's charter schools are falling behind those in other states, according to a new report released this week.

In fact, the commission found that not only are charter schools in Utah not meeting performance standards for achievement, but 18 of the state's non-charter schools scored better.

"We're all concerned with the gap of improvement," said Chad Mason, the executive director of the commission.

"We will continue to work on our charter school performance, and we will anticipate that we'll be able to close that gap," he said.

From all publicly available data, the state's charter schools saw an average 3.7 percent decline in test scores over the last three years.

The commission said charter schools overall need more professional development and teachers.

"We're not seeing the same level of professional development being provided every year so it's not necessarily the teachers' fault because there's not enough available funding," said Mason.

text_original:
A class action suit has been filed by a Birmingham Alabama woman that accuses Apple of deceptive advertising in their claim that the 3G iPhone is "twice as fast" as the previous.

According to court documents, plaintiff Virginia Davis, who purchased her iPhone 3G on January 16th, 2010, is asking for over $5,000 in damages for false advertising. Davis, a Birmingham resident, bought her iPhone 3G for $479 at AT&T, and is currently paying an additional $20 a month for data services which she said was "not worth it".

Davis believes that Apple's advertisement of the iPhone 3G's "up to twice as fast" performance was misleading because it does not take into account the limitations of the 3G's 4G connection.

"The video of the 'up to twice as fast' claim appears to show the iPhone 3G running at 4G speed, but it was shot on a 3G network," the complaint states. "When viewing the video on a 3G network, the iPhone 3G turns into a slow, laggy device, and should only be seen as a phone capable of using 4G speeds."

" When viewing the video on a 3 G network , the iPhone 3 G turns into a slow , laggy device , and should only be seen as a phone capable of using 4 G speeds . "

Filed by a Birmingham Alabama woman, a class action suit accuses Apple of deceptive advertising in their claim that the 3 G iPhone is " twice as fast " as the previous. According to court documents , plaintiff Virginia Davis , who purchased her iPhone 3 G on January 16th , 2010 , is asking for over $ 5,000 in damages for false advertising . Davis , a Birmingham resident , bought her iPhone 3 G for $ 479 at AT&T , and is currently paying an additional $ 20 a month for data services which she said was " not worth it " . Davis believes that Apple 's advertisement of the iPhone 3 G 's " up to twice as fast " performance was misleading because it does not take into account the limitations of the 3 G 's 4 G connection . " The video of the ' up to twice as fast ' claim appears to show the iPhone 3 G running at 4 G speed , but it was shot on a 3 G network , " the complaint states . " When viewing the video on a 3 G network , the iPhone 3 G turns into a slow , laggy device , and should only be seen as a phone capable of using 4 G speeds . "The iPhone 3 G should only be seen as a phone capable of using 4 G speeds, and when viewing the video on a 3 G network, it turns into a slow, laggy device."

text_original:
Your credit card company doesn't have your best interests at heart.  **Do not** pay any of this back until you have a credit card with no annual fee, and a history of good payments.
They are a big company. I don't think they would have the best interests in me, but who knows.   What exactly do you mean by "no annual fee, and a history of good payments"?
You should start saving up for a new credit card once that one is paid off (or even if it's paid off yet).  You've got a lot of debt, and it won't be easy to get rid of all the credit card debt if you keep paying off the same card every month.  It'll take time, but it'll be worth it in the end.
I did not think about that. I am definitely going to start doing that. Thank you!
No problem.  Don't forget about the higher interest rates on purchases.  That's usually the main reason for getting a credit card, so it's worth thinking about.

Sascha Segan MiFi 500 LTE by Novatel Wireless (Sprint) The Sprint MiFi 500 is a solid workhorse wireless hotspot for Sprint's new tri-band LTE network. The LTE-only MiFi 500 is compatible with up to three devices and offers blazing fast speeds of up to 9 Mbps download and up to 3 Mbps upload. Available in white, black, and silver, the MiFi 500 runs off Sprint's 1xRTT technology and supports both UMTS and LTE networks in the United States. It's also supported in Canada and Australia.

text_original:
The Reporter brought home six journalism awards from NYPA’s spring convention — including top prizes in three categories — this past weekend at the Gideon Putnam Resort and Spa in Saratoga Springs.
The top prize in the headline writing category was awarded to the New York State Newspaper Publishers Association for the Charlie Parr story, “How the pandemic devastated the region’s Black community.” The award was presented by Associated Press President and CEO Gary Pruitt.
Also earning top honors in the headline writing category were the story by John Clay, “NYSCuomo’s scandalous legacy lives on,” and the story by Jacob Becherer, “How to get away with murder: They were all cops.”
The award-winning work of reporter Daniel Zingaro in the investigative reporting category was recognized by the executive committee of the NYSPNA.
The Spotlight Award for Excellence in Reporting went to the Times Union for “A Stakeholder Perspective on the Governor’s handling of nursing homes COVID-19 Deaths.” The award was presented by AP President and CEO Gary Pruitt.
Maria Lasky of the Syracuse Post-Standard won the top prize in the feature writing category for her story, “A One-Way Street: Grieving a Black man who died in a police shooting,” which was published in The Post-Standard in December 2020.

text_original:
I have to admit – I’m a huge breakfast person. In the morning I’m usually up before my alarm, and I’m not a big fan of coffee or tea. I don’t like sweet things but love savory ones, and I most definitely hate fruit. If you’re a morning person like me, there are some things that you shouldn’t skip.

While there are many different ways to prepare bacon, my favourite is to make strips that are thinly sliced, pan fried, and topped with cheese, maple syrup, and butter.

Since this is my weekend, I’ve made this recipe twice now, and I love it even more each time. I’m not sure if it’s because I’m in love with bacon strips, or because they’re so easy to make, but this is one of my favourite recipes.

The bacon is really crispy on the outside and soft and fluffy on the inside, and the maple syrup adds a nice nutty flavour.

text_original:
Responding to every bit of Russian propaganda "is counterproductive, because it is reactive and you are always behind the curve,” Deputy Assistant Secretary of State Benjamin Ziff tells senators in Washington, Nov. 17

The Internet Research Agency (IRA) is a troll farm that ran an online campaign against Hillary Clinton and President Barack Obama during the 2016 U.S. presidential election.

On Nov. 17, the Russian government denied the allegations in a statement released by the Kremlin, but that doesn't stop people from trying to paint the picture.

In an open letter to senators, Benjamin Ziff, the deputy assistant secretary of state for European and Eurasian Affairs, says that responding to every single propaganda tweet is counterproductive given the nature of the Russian communications.

"Replies to every single tweet of the IRA's will only generate more propaganda," Ziff said, adding that the U.S. government can't always catch up with Russian propaganda.

Read the full letter here:

This is a developing story.

WATCH: RT Australia correspondent Kevin Parkin speaks to RT America editor-at-large Steven Erlanger about the latest developments in the United States following US intelligence's conclusion that Russia meddled in the 2016 presidential election.

CELEBRITY mums Billie Faiers and Abbey Clancy are loving this affordable toy.

12

WENN

Abbey Clancy and Billie Faiers.

The former Made In Chelsea stars have been loving the new Play Smart Playhouse by Lego, which has a price tag of just £20 and comes with a 30-minute playtime activity.

The two youngsters were so impressed they even asked Lego for a copy when it's released on September 30.

Lego have even made their own version of the playhouse with a built-in tablet and a remote control.

12

WENN

Billie Faiers and Abbey Clancy.

Lego said it is "designed to be an educational toy that helps children learn about the natural world".

"It is also fun for children to build, from the beginning, with bricks and Lego, through to the end and see how their creations grow."

Twin sisters Daisy and Betty, four, have already watched their mum build her own LEGO playhouse and thought it was great.

text_original:
When it comes to corruption and Congress, the line the law polices is the wrong line, writes Lawrence Lessig.

(Image by Lawrence Lessig) Details DMCA

On August 7, the Supreme Court heard oral arguments on a case that echoes throughout the history of our nation. The case involves the use of the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) by Secretary of Defense Donald Rumsfeld in connection with the procurement of a controversial Saudi Arabian oil company, Saudis Aramco. The issue in the case is whether a foreign government's ownership of, or influence over, an American company can be used as a motive to influence the acquisition of U.S. goods and services by the U.S. government.

The question posed by the case is, "Is a foreign government's ownership of, or influence over, an American company, or the companies within its ownership, a crime when it's used to influence the acquisition of U.S. goods and services?"

In the past 40 years or so, the FCPA has evolved from a tool to prosecute foreign bribery by corporations, to a weapon to prosecute foreign governments that buy U.S. goods and services through corrupt means.

text_original:
Business, medical, construction and development interests have poured money into the gubernatorial campaign of Republican Robert Bentley, while labor unions and PACs that received contributions from gambling have been a major source.

The latest data from the Federal Election Commission:

The Bentley campaign raised $2.8 million in the second quarter, bringing its total to $4.1 million. The campaign has spent $2.5 million on advertising and $1.1 million on fundraising.

Bentley has been aided by a $1 million loan from Bentley’s campaign and another $900,000 from political action committees controlled by the governor’s wife, former Birmingham Mayor Tommy Battle, that could be forgiven if he wins the election.

The Bentley campaign also is expected to raise more money on the Aug. 30 primary election, when voters will choose between Bentley and two Democrats seeking to replace him as governor.

Bentley’s biggest financial backers are two groups that control Alabama’s largest business coalition — the Chamber of Commerce and the Business Council for Alabama.

The Chamber of Commerce gave Bentley a $500,000 loan. The Business Council for Alabama contributed $400,000.

text_original:
The release of 26 Palestinian prisoners and the approval of West Bank settlement expansions has both Israelis and Palestinians crying foul.

Palestinians are mourning the loss of their loved ones who were killed by Israeli soldiers in a wave of violent clashes that began in late March.

Israeli officials say the decision to free the 26 prisoners was part of the government’s long-term plans to reduce the detention pool and encourage more transfers.

“This is part of a long-term plan to reduce the number of detainees,” said Israel’s Justice Ministry spokesman Ronen Bergman.

Palestinians view the release of the prisoner as a sign of weakness.

“We don’t know what will happen when we initiate the return of the prisoners back to their villages or towns,” said Alia Mansour, an official from Hamas, the Islamist movement that operates in the Gaza Strip.

The prisoners were captured by the Israeli military during the 2014 war on Hamas.

Nearly 100,000 Palestinians remain under Israeli occupation, according to the U.N. Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs.

text_original:
Nearly 50 years of protests, lawsuits and ballot battles over low-income housing have made San Francisco a nationwide model for efforts to keep urban America from becoming home to only the very.

Karen Long is president of the Asian Pacific Policy and Planning Council, a liberal advocacy group that has been pushing for affordable housing at San Francisco’s high-rises for nearly three decades.

Her group has been trying to get federal funds for affordable housing in the city ever since the 1970s.

But like so many others, it was stymied by powerful groups who paid off elected officials and regulators.

Yet, as the city continues to struggle to meet its housing needs, the long-held frustration of developers is becoming a rallying cry for activists.

CALLING TO ACTION

When she was first elected mayor in 1989, Bill Graham said affordable housing was “one of the most important issues” he would address.

He began a process that led to the creation of the Department of Housing and Urban Development, which began awarding federal funds to the city in 1994.

In 1999, the city won a $6.2 million grant to build an affordable housing complex on the corner of Fillmore and Post Streets.

text_original:
Some independent experts say DNA testing is inadequate for analyzing botanical products.

New research suggests that dozens of types of cannabis could be dangerous for people with certain genetic conditions and disorders.

Scientists have been using DNA testing to determine what strains of cannabis are safe and effective for years. But some experts have argued that there's little evidence that the testing takes into account certain genetic conditions.

Now, a new study suggests that as many as 33 types of "cannabis" could pose health risks for people with conditions such as epilepsy, autism, and schizophrenia. The findings were published in the journal PLOS ONE on Tuesday.

"The results are very alarming," said Dr. Brian Giese, a professor of medicine at the University of California, San Francisco, who wasn't involved in the study. "These are real concerns."

"For me, it was very disturbing," he added. "These are real concerns."

Giese has worked with people with epilepsy and other conditions for 20 years.

Dr. Timothy Rayfield, a professor of medicine at the University of California, Davis Medical Center, agreed.

"

text_original:
One week after a dog spooked by fireworks was hit by a SkyTrain, resident Bruce Mitchell is asking the City of Vancouver to revisit its fireworks bylaws.
On Sunday, Mitchell said his dog was inside his home in the 8000 block of Marine Drive when an explosion sent fireworks into the air.
"It just went off," he said. "I was watching TV and then I heard this huge bang."
The dog ran out of the house, and Mitchell followed it, but he wasn't sure exactly what happened until he saw the animal stuck in the window of the house.
"When I went outside there was this big hole in the window. The dog got stuck in it," he said. "I'm not sure how many fireworks there were, but I think they were between two and three."
Mitchell said the fireworks struck the dog badly enough that it had to be put down.
"This dog was affected really bad," he said. "He's going to have some issues with his vision for life because of the fireworks."
Mitchell said he doesn't know what the dog was scared of, but that he thinks fireworks are a problem in the city.
"

text_original:
The US economy “remains strong”, the Federal Reserve chairman, Jerome Powell, told Congress on Tuesday as he signaled the central bank is on course to carry on gradually increasing interest rates in.

In a speech delivered at the Cleveland Fed, Powell said the central bank remains “confident” in the strength of the US economy and that the “relatively soft” inflation data “has not changed our assessment of the outlook”.

Powell also said that the US economic expansion has continued into its 11th year and will likely continue for some time.

It is a reference to the fact the Fed’s policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is forecasting 2.7% growth this year and 2.8% in 2019. In June, it raised its benchmark rate by a quarter point to 1.5%.

The FOMC is also forecasting 3.9% growth in 2020 and 2.9% in 2021.

“We remain confident in the strength of the US economy, which continues to expand at a moderate pace, with employment growth strong, inflation moderately high, and growth in real output balanced by a tight labor market,” Powell said.

Powell also said that the US economic expansion has continued into its 11th year and will likely continue for some time . It is a reference to the fact the Fed ’s policy - setting Federal Open Market Committee ( FOMC ) is forecasting 2.7 % growth this year and 2.8 % in 2019 . In June , it raised its benchmark rate by a quarter point to 1.5 % . The FOMC is also forecasting 3.9 % growth in 2020 and 2.9 % in 2021 . “Confident in the strength of the US economy, we remain, with strong employment growth, a moderate pace of expansion, inflation moderately high, and balanced growth in real output by a tight labor market,” Powell said.

Bigg Boss 12 contestants: Jasleen Mathur is said to be dating Bhajan singer Anup Jalota.

Bigg Boss 12 is a reality show which is hosted by Salman Khan. The contestants of the show are given t-shirts with the tricolour and the logo of the show. As usual, Salman brings in lots of new contestants who have no prior association with Bigg Boss.

Recently, it was reported that Bigg Boss 12 contestants including Parth Samthaan, Jasleen Mathur, Ranvir Shorey, Punit Pathak, Ratna Pathak Shah and Mandana Karimi will stop singing the song 'Teri mausam' and will sing a new version of the same.

In the upcoming episode, an adjustment room is also made for the new contestants. Also, there will be another meeting in the house wherein Jaan Kumar Sanu will be seen playing the role of the 'Kena' or the Bigg Boss Room Manager.

There will surely be many twists and turns in the upcoming episodes of Bigg Boss 12. The current season will go on air from October 3.

Parents with children in tow stagger onto a waiting train at Donaghmede Dart station. Picture: RTE

THE MOST hard-pressed parents with children in tow, who are trying to reach Dublin Airport, have been left stranded after they were forced to wait at Donaghmede Dart station for three hours on Monday night.

The disruption was caused by overcrowding on trains heading to the airport, said an RTE reporter who was on board one of the trains.

Severe overcrowding also affected customers at Donnybrook, with some passengers on board being left stranded for up to six hours. A spokesman for Dublin Bus said there had been “a number of problems” with overcrowding on services to Donnybrook that evening and into the morning.

text_original:
Migrants use wifi and a hub for plugging in their smartphones at a temporary shelter for migrants opened this week in a hall of the Berliner Messe trade fair grounds on October. A reception center for asylum seekers and refugees with up to 50 beds opened in the same building on Monday. The Federal Association for Immigration and Integration (BSI) has said it expects the number of asylum applications to increase from 27,000 in the first three months of 2015 to 45,000 by the end of the year.

The human-rights organization Amnesty International has called on Germany to stop enforcing a new law that makes it easier for migrants to avoid deportation with just a letter from a doctor saying they are suffering from mental illness. Amnesty International has been warning for months that the new law, proposed last month by Interior Minister Thomas de Maiziere, will “undermine the basic rights” of asylum seekers and refugees, and “sends out the wrong message” about the country.

The same day Amnesty International published its report, Germany’s federal immigration agency, the BSI, said it had received 9,500 asylum applications, and would process 8,900 of them by the end of this month.

text_original:
'Black Panther''s box office dominance continues as the Marvel blockbuster passed 'Titanic' to become the third highest-grossing film of all time.

The superhero flick's worldwide gross of $959.1 million (HK$10.6 billion) is the fifth-highest total ever, behind only 'Star Wars, 'Godzilla, 'Avatar' and 'Raiders of the Lost Ark.''

According to data from Box Office Mojo, the film also surpassed 'Titanic' to become the third-highest-grossing film of all time.

However, with current inflation, 'Black Panther''s global gross is still less than 'Titanic''s $1.13 billion (HK$15.8 billion) — the best-known film in history.

'Black Panther,' which was released domestically in January 2018, grossed $117 million from North American theaters in its first 10 days and went on to grab $387.3 million domestically.

It then took the top spot in North America for a record 10 weeks, earning $945.2 million, before taking over the throne in the United States.

text_original:
A message in a bottle that washed up more than 108 years after it was thrown into the sea may be the world's oldest, a marine association has said.

The bottle contains a fragment of a rock crystal and a message: "Ladies and gentleman, we have found a piece of history".

The discovery was made during an oceanographic expedition led by Dr Peter Lopresti of the University of Cambridge.

The message was discovered on the seafloor near the North West Shelf in 2001.

It is thought to date back to before 1814, when the British Crown sold much of the last of its possessions off the coast of Scotland to buy the islands.

The British Crown sold much of the last of its possessions off the coast of Scotland to buy the islands

Dr Lopresti and his team believe the message was written in the early part of the 19th Century by someone who knew they would never see it again.

They say it is the oldest ever written message ever recovered from the seabed.

The Royal Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (RSPCA) will be able to use the discovery to help identify the species of fossil sea urchin that lived in the area at the time.

text_original:
“God’s Plan” was named the Best Rap Song.

The Grammy Awards were held at the Staples Center in Los Angeles on Sunday, February 10, and Rihanna performed “God’s Plan” with Migos and DJ Khaled. She dedicated her performance to fans, saying, “For every girl who has ever been hurt by someone else, thank you. For every man out there who has ever been in love with a girl, thank you.”

Rihanna also won the Best Rap/Sung Collaboration award for her collaboration with Travis Scott on “SICKO MODE.” The song also won Best Rap Song, Best Rap Performance and Best Rap Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocals.

Her track “Work” won Best Rap/Sung Collaboration, and the song also won Best Rap Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocals.

She also won Album of the Year, Record of the Year, Song of the Year, Best Rap Song and Best Rap Performance by a Duo or Group with Vocals.

Gay nephew might want for his aunt to advise him or intercede on his behalf with his dad.  Or maybe he'll be so gay, he'll tell his mom about it and her and dad will have an on-going discussion about it.
Yeah fuck that. I'm not having my aunt take the side of this guy.
> Yeah fuck that.  What about your aunt?
She's married to my Uncle. She's just cool. She doesn't do anything wrong, and she doesn't deserve to be told about my sexuality by someone else who isn't even involved in my life.
Let's be real, if you were a woman, you'd be doing the same thing.
I don't know why you're getting downvoted. People here are either drones that upvote everything they read, or idiots who have no clue what they're talking about.
It's just the way reddit is. It's always the minority who gets downvotes.  Edit: My original comment was about how a lot of people are getting downvoted here, not the other post.

Venezuela's opposition leader Juan Guaido is trying to get millions of dollars in humanitarian aid into his country despite a blockade from disputed President Nicolas Maduro; State Department correspondent Rich Edson reports.

RACHEL MARTIN, HOST:

The U.S. and a handful of other countries have recognized Venezuelan opposition leader Juan Guaido as the country's interim president. So far, the Trump administration has given $15 million in aid to Venezuela but says it will be blocked by the Venezuelan government. NPR's James Jimenez is in Caracas, where there are long lines at main points for food and medicine.

Hi, Jimenez.

JAMES JIMEMEX, BYLINE: Hi, Rachel.

MARTIN: I understand that the U.S. is planning on sending more aid, and the EU is considering doing so as well. What kinds of supplies - what types of supplies are being sent?

JIMEMEX: Yeah, so the U.S. is helping deliver a small amount of relief to Venezuelans who are already suffering. And the European Union is also giving money to Venezuela to pay off its debts to the international community.

The Narromine Garden Club have gifted their annual donation to local organisation the Narromine Volunteer Rescue Association (VRA).

The Narromine Garden Club have gifted their annual donation to local organisation the Narromine Volunteer Rescue Association (VRA).

“It is a pleasure when the Narromine Garden Club can contribute to a worthy cause,” said Club member Irene Sweeney.

“Our aim is to raise funds for the VRA and each year we have been able to do that through our annual flower sale and also through donations from members of the club,” she said.

The club has donated more than $5000 in the past five years and this year's donation included hundreds of rose bushes.

“A big thank you to everyone who has donated to the VRA this year,” Mrs Sweeney said.

The VRA's annual fundraiser was held last Thursday at Narromine Library with a lively dance competition and stalls, food and beverages, live music, an auction and raffle.

“We were very happy to receive this donation and the funds will go towards assisting the VRA with its work,” Ms Sweeney said.

text_original:
Kip Hagopian is the chairman of Maxim Integrated Products Inc. and the former president of Intel Corp. He will step down from his roles on Aug. 31.

Hagopian, who in 2010 was named Ernst & Young’s “Man of the Year” for his work on Intel’s acquisition of Novell, is leaving the chipmaker after a decade at the helm.

The company said in a statement that he will remain with the company until an external search is completed.

Hagopian was appointed chairman in 2009 following the departure of Scott McNealy. He replaced McNealy as CEO in August 2011.

Intel shares were down more than 2% before the opening bell Wednesday.

The company said it plans to fill the role with an outside candidate later this month.

Hagopian worked at Intel for more than three decades before joining Novell in 1987. He left Novell in 1998 to join Hewlett-Packard and then Intel.

He has been credited with helping push through the purchase of Novell.

text_original:
Vicki Denton, who on vacation to Orlando from Ohio, perused items in the gift shop at Hollywood Studios when the alleged incident occurred in 2016.

Henry County Sheriff’s Office detectives arrested Ricky Johnson, 57, of Pittsburgh, and charged him with one count of aggravated battery resulting in serious bodily injury.

According to an arrest report, Johnson was riding a motorcycle southbound westbound on Hollywood Boulevard at about 6:30 p.m. on July 30, 2016 when he crossed into the path of Denton.

“I was looking for something in the store and I tripped over a rock and I fell,” Denton told deputies. “I saw the bike coming and I tried to get out of the way. It was dark so I couldn’t see the bike.”

“I do remember seeing the light because I kept looking at my phone to see if I had a text message,” she continued. “I didn’t. I don’t think it was really reflective or anything so I didn’t see the bike until it was too late.”

Johnson reportedly stopped and then rode back toward his bike.
