text_original:
Islamabad: Effective tourism strategies can contribute a lot to strengthen Pakistan’s economy and improve its international image and the key industry players are taking steps for it.

Pakistan’s tourism sector is going through a difficult time when the number of visits has declined due to terrorist attacks.

The country has not been as active as it was before. However, the recent travel ban imposed by some countries on Pakistani citizens has further affected the industry.

Saleem Chaudhry, Chairman and CEO, Tangerine Hotels & Resorts, said that there are two sides to every story and that the two sides are being presented by different media.

He said that Tangerine Group is trying to take resort operations to Canada and the UK. “Although the market is restricted and the domestic market is quite slow, the company is making plans for international expansion.”

He said that foreign trips in the country have been declining lately due to apprehensions over terrorist attacks, but the current situation in the country has seen a rise in tourists.

“We have seen a big increase in tourist arrivals from India and Dubai since the ban was imposed,” he told APP.

Buddhadev had written a strong letter of protest to Manmohan Singh objecting to Mulford's behaviour. "I have been given no other explanation for the behaviour of Mulford than that he is an obnoxious and damaging character," Buddhadev said in his letter, referring to the former finance minister as 'Narasimha Rao' after the latter's death in 2006.

"In the last few weeks, I have received more than 100 letters protesting Singh's behaviour against me. I have not received any reply or explanation from him apart from one or two remarks in one or two letters on the subject," Buddhadev said.

In his letter, Buddhadev said Mulford's attitude towards him was not only hostile but also hurtful.

"Saying that he has been made aware of my views and wishes, he wrote to me in an unkind way that he had better make a new friend," Buddhadev said.

"He also said that I should be well aware that I have not changed and must justify my views on the various issues," he added.

text_original:
Two officers of a company that operates three Manhattan hotels were indicted yesterday in a scheme to help homeless people fraudulently obtain welfare checks and split the money with the hotels.

The men, identified as John T. Menendez, a vice president of Metropolitan Hospitality, and Leonard J. Edelman, a director of the company, are accused of paying homeless people to set up fake “conditions” in their hotel rooms while they were on interstate or foreign travel, the Manhattan District Attorney’s Office said. The payments continued after the hotels’ owners learned of the “conditions.”

The men also allegedly used the hotels’ credit cards to purchase airline tickets and hotel stay essentials, then deposited the money into the accounts of their relatives and friends, some of whom were homeless themselves.

Manhattan District Attorney Cyrus Vance Jr. said his office was “proud to prosecute these two brokers for helping to defraud the government.”

In a statement, Mr. Vance called the operation “a brazen scheme, which according to the indictment, cost millions of dollars in public benefits to individuals who were never eligible.”

Mr. Menendez and Mr. Edelman are charged with grand larceny, conspiracy to commit grand larceny, and several counts of falsifying business records.

text_original:
In their first game since dropping out of the top five, the Irish delivered a redemption performance against Boston College, picking up a 50-point win over the Eagles while simultaneously moving one.

"We're just trying to do everything we can to win, and we did that," senior guard Mike Watkins said. "We had a good night offensively and defensively. It was great to see us come together as a team and play like we did."

The Irish went into halftime trailing by 10 points and never trailed again. The defense forced four straight turnovers on BC's first possession of the second half to start the comeback, then put together a 15-0 run to finish off the victory.

"We try to remind ourselves that we need to respect defense in this league," Watkins said. "That's usually the first thing we focus on. We didn't do that tonight, so we got to do that better."

The Irish had no trouble with the Eagles, who are coming off a 3-5 start and have dropped eight of their last 10 games. The Irish led by as many as 34 before running out of steam in the second half.

On the other side of the court, UCSB (4-9) had some trouble with Notre Dame, too.

text_original:
DPR members Jim Ragsdale, Diane Kane, Angeles Liera and pro tem chair Mike Costello discuss condo conversion projects.

City Councilmember Mike Costello emphasized that the council needs to look at the impact of these projects on the city’s fiscal situation.

“We need to be very careful about the way we do this,” he said. “The impact of large development projects is a difficult thing to quantify, but I think it’s fair to say — we know there are a lot of projects happening, and this is going to have an impact on the budget.”

Councilmember Jim Ragsdale was one of the only councilmembers who voiced an opposition to the projects. He said he wanted to see more details from the developers involved with the projects before supporting them, and he felt the council wasn’t doing enough to get the details right.

Ragsdale said he had heard from the developers involved that the projects were good for the city, but he didn’t hear that they were good for the city financially.

“So I’m not convinced that we’re getting it right,” he said.

text_original:
Everybody knows the parable of the man who built his house on sand.
Actually, it's the parable of the guy who built his house on a rock.
The man who built his house on a rock, is not the same as the man who built his house on sand.  The man who built his house on a rock, was a fool, but he was no dummy.   Edit:  I'm sorry I offended you, I am not a Christian and was just trying to be funny.  I don't know why it came across as an insult.
You might want to reread the story.  It's not a parable about being a fool.
If you and I were to have a discussion about the meaning of the parable of the man who built his house on a rock, what would we talk about?  What would you tell me?    If I said "The man who built his house on a rock, was a fool, but he was no dummy," what would you say?    (I'm not saying this guy was a fool, I'm not saying this guy was not a fool.)

text_original:
The rules in soccer are pretty clear-cut: When a player removes his shirt during a goal celebration, it is an automatic yellow card. However, with the World Cup just around the corner, some players are taking it to the extreme and wearing their shirts as a flag.

These include left-back Leonardo Bonucci, who wore a shirt that read “No Jacket No Shirt” during Juventus’ 4-1 win over Cagliari on Sunday. The message was also written on the back of the shirt, which was later removed by the club’s marketing department.

Also on rt.com ‘No Jacket No Shirt’: Cristiano Ronaldo on FIFA World Cup red card in Russia for shirtless celebration (VIDEO)

The shirt is not the first time the Italian defender has worn a shirt without a shirt. He previously sported a shirt with the slogan ‘Silhoutte,’ or ‘silence.’

Bonucci was also booked for his shirtless celebration during Juve’s 5-0 win over Sampdoria on Monday.

Included among these is left-back Leonardo Bonucci, who during Juventus' 4-1 win over Cagliari on Sunday wore a shirt that read “No Jacket No Shirt.” The back of the shirt also had the message written on it, which was later removed by the club's marketing department. The shirt is not the first time the Italian defender has worn a shirt without a shirt . He previously sported a shirt with the slogan ‘ Silhoutte , ’ or ‘ silence . ’ Bonucci was also booked for his shirtless celebration during Juve ’s 5 - 0 win over Sampdoria on Monday .

Unfortunately, Sir James Robertson’s plan succeeded partly because leading southern politicians, especially Dr. Richard Nixon, played a key role in the scheme. As a result of Nixon’s efforts, Congressman Earl Dole was able to convince President Johnson to change course and allow the U.S. to exit the union.

What was it that led to this eventual success?

The answer is as complex as the myriad of reasons and motives behind it. According to historian Charles Burns, it could be summed up as a combination of factors:

1) The influence of southern politicians.

2) The influence of northern politicians.

3) The influence of the Supreme Court.

4) Nationalism.

5) The generosity of the Southern states

In other words, those two forces together made the South a practical political force.

The High Cost of Union keeps us talking about the Civil War.

But we don’t talk enough about the cost of the war to each generation of Americans.

As historian Edwin W. Burck says, “We need to remember the high cost of the war to all of us–to our families, to our children, to our grandchildren, and beyond.” If you really want to understand what caused the war, you have to learn more about the cost of the war to each generation.

text_original:
Investors in Lexicon Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ:LXRX) are going crazy after the company reported better-than-expected second quarter earnings and raised its full-year guidance. Shares of the biotech company jumped more than 33% in extended trading.

Lexicon has been developing a new class of drugs for the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases, including Alzheimer’s Disease. The Alzheimer’s disease market is estimated to be worth more than $40 billion by 2024, according to Stat.

Today, Lexicon announced it had entered into a definitive agreement with Vertex Pharmaceuticals for the acquisition of its U.S. rights to a drug that treats neuropathic pain. The drug, Eylea, was approved in June 2014. It was also approved for use as an adjunct therapy to opioids in patients with post-operative pain.

Lexicon said it expects to initiate dosing for the Phase III study with Eylea in the third quarter of 2016.

The company also today announced the launch of a new clinical program to evaluate the efficacy of Lexicon’s Sotatercept in treating patients with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA).

text_original:
Donald Bren, chairman of the Irvine Co.’s board of directors, has been named a finalist for the prestigious Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year award, the company said Thursday.
Bren, who became chairman of the Irvine Co. board in December, is among the finalists for the award, which is given to a young innovator under 35. The winner will be announced at the EY Entrepreneur of the Year in Business event on April 6 in New York.
Bren has led the Irvine Co. since it was the Irvine Company in 2004. A longtime Los Angeles resident, Bren moved his family to Costa Mesa in 2013 and became president of Irvine Co. that same year. The company is one of the largest privately held real estate companies in Southern California.
In addition to his title as chairman of the Irvine Co., Bren also serves on the boards of the University of Southern California, UCLA and the Irvine Company.
Bren earned his Bachelor of Arts degree from the University of Southern California and a Juris Doctor degree from the University of California, Los Angeles.

text_original:
U by Uniworld has said its ship, The B, will be moving to central Europe for the 2020 season. The brand has hired a chief executive officer and a new president of Asia-Pacific.

The move, which is set to take place in early 2020, follows a recent refit of the vessel.

Uniworld is a subsidiary of Uniworld Group, which owns the brand Uniworld.

U by Uniworld was founded in the 1970s as a family business, selling school uniforms and accessories in Australia and New Zealand.

In the 2000s, Uniworld began expanding into the fashion retail sector with the launch of the U brand, which offers high-end clothing under the Uniworld brand name.

In 2009, Uniworld began investing in the Asia-Pacific market, acquiring a majority share in Moda Operandi, which operates retail stores in Brazil, Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam and Singapore.

Following the investment, Uniworld’s focus on the Asia-Pacific region has seen the brand expand its store network in the region, including the opening of stores in Japan and Australia.

text_original:
Here's some of what's happening this morning in entertainment headlines: Selena Gomez drops an F-bomb; Meredith Baxter marries; and more.

Selena Gomez

The singer-songwriter has had a rough couple of weeks, but she seems to be back on track with her career. In an interview with People magazine, Gomez says that she’s looking forward to releasing her new album later this year. “I have so much stuff planned out, but right now I just kind of want to take a breath and regroup and get my head straight,” she said. “I can’t wait for everyone to hear it.”

Selena Gomez has some advice for her fans.

“It’s OK to not be OK,” she told the magazine. “Just make sure you’re OK.”

Meredith Baxter, 30

Baxter married her longtime boyfriend, John Merrow, last weekend at the top of the Grand Canyon. The couple was first linked in April 2015, when they were seen romancing on a yacht trip in Miami Beach, Fla.

Meredith Baxter and John Merrow

Baxter was previously married to actor Hugh Jackman, who she met during a trip to see him perform in Australia in 2011.

Baxter was previously married to actor Hugh Jackman, whom she met during a trip to see him perform in Australia in 2011.

text_original:
Did van Gogh cut off his ear because his brother was engaged?
It's the ear of a dead person.  Edgy.
I don't think it's edgy. I think it's very interesting and thought provoking. It's a rare subject, that is rather morbid to discuss.
How is it morbid?
I think it's morbid because every time you hear about it or see a picture of it, it reminds you of someone who's no longer here.
Well, then why are you on this sub?
Because all of us should at least try and be aware of what happened and try and avoid it in the future.
So, how does this post make you aware of what happened?
I think it's relevant to the sub even if it doesn't make you aware of what happened.
So, then why are you on this sub?
To discuss things in a more responsible manner than people on some other subs do.
So, how does this post make you aware of what happened?
I think it's relevant to the sub even if it doesn't make you aware of what happened.

Rep. Steve Scalise, R-La., plans to use a procedural tactic to force a floor vote on a bill that would punish any doctor who fails to provide medical care to a child.

“We need to make sure the American people know if their doctor or a hospital is refusing to treat a child because of their sex or sexual orientation,” Scalise said in a statement Wednesday evening. “That’s why I will be introducing legislation in the House to require every licensed health care provider to report this information.”

The bill would require a doctor to report information for at least two years, and the doctor must report it to federal authorities annually, according to Scalise’s office.

Scalise cited an article published by the Washington Post last month about a doctor who refused to treat a lesbian couple from Chicago because of his religious beliefs.

“Even though I am not against healthcare providers deciding what to do with their patients based on their personal beliefs, this is a serious violation of fundamental rights and a threat to our nation’s healthcare system,” Scalise said in a statement.

Scalise’s move comes as Republicans are taking steps to discredit President Barack Obama’s signature health care law, which includes protections for gay and transgender Americans.

Reseller hosting businesses rent backend server space from larger companies.  If you don't have the money to buy them outright, you could rent a server for cheap enough that you might be able to make money off of it.    Depending on how big your business is, or how much money you have to spend, this can be a good way to get an initial start.
i will check this out. thank you!
I would recommend you don't start a reseller hosting business until you have a solid idea of what you want to do with it.   You can have a reseller hosting business make you some money, but you can also have a reseller hosting business fail miserably and lose more than you ever made.    It's best to plan early on so you don't waste time and money.    You may need to start a full-on, 5-figure company to get a reseller hosting business off the ground.
That's true. I was planning to start a reseller hosting company, but there are a lot of things to consider before that.

text_original:
It recently dawned on me that it has been nearly nine long years since my wife and children moved back to India. I feel as though I have been missing out on the most important part of my life for the last decade.

I am fortunate to have lived in the United States for most of my life. I have traveled, seen places, experienced things that no one will ever be able to do. I wrote about a lot of that in my post India: A Tale of Two Countries.

But if I were to review my life from the point of view of the Indian people, I would find myself at a loss for words. I can’t begin to tell you how much I miss it. It was a dream come true.

In this post, I want to talk about one of the most challenging aspects of being an American expat living in India…the culture shock.

It is easy to become accustomed to the surroundings. I always felt at home in India. There was no fear of being attacked or robbed. There were no cars that I couldn’t drive. It was easy to get around.

We've spent a lot of time covering IK Multimedia's line of iOS accessories for guitarists. The new iVirtual Guitar Starter Kit is the latest tool to help beginners get started and we like what we see.

The iVirtual Guitar Starter Kit is part of a larger family of iVirtual products that includes the iVirtual Guitar Master Trainer, iVirtual Guitar Solo Trainer, iVirtual Guitar Tuner, and iVirtual Guitar Performance Trainer. All are helpful in getting better at playing music, but this one takes the cake for a few reasons.

First, it's more convenient than ever to get a gig, whether it be on stage or in a room with friends. Second, the kit comes with a couple of tools that will make your touring life easier.

The iVirtual Guitar Master Trainer

The iVirtual Guitar Master Trainer is an online multi-angle guitar instructor that helps you learn how to play your instrument. It's the only product in the iVirtual Guitar lineup to come with a built-in microphone to record yourself playing.

The iVirtual Guitar Master Trainer lets you do everything from standing in the corner of the room, recording yourself, to recording yourself playing in front of a room full of people.

We 've spent a lot of time covering IK Multimedia 's line of iOS accessories for guitarists . We like what we see, and the new iVirtual Guitar Starter Kit is the latest tool to help beginners get started. A larger family of iVirtual products that includes the iVirtual Guitar Master Trainer , iVirtual Guitar Solo Trainer , iVirtual Guitar Tuner , and iVirtual Guitar Performance Trainer is part of the iVirtual Guitar Starter Kit . This one takes the cake for a few reasons , but all are helpful in getting better at playing music . First , it 's more convenient than ever to get a gig , whether it be on stage or in a room with friends . The kit comes with a couple of tools that will make your touring life easier, second. I imagine that Ellis would find this reaction delightful.
As much as I love and respect you, I have to disagree here.  I'm a huge fan of yours, but I'm not your fan.  And while I'm sure you'll be able to appreciate the humor, it's pretty cheesy to say "you're not my fan" in a roundabout way like this.    Keep up the good work though, Mr. Ellis, you are an amazing writer!
> and while I'm sure you'll be able to appreciate the humor, it's pretty cheesy to say "you're not my fan" in a roundabout way like this.  It's not really round-about, it's just kind of dismissive.  I mean, have you ever even met him?
"I'm not his fan" simply means "I don't care for him."  If you dislike someone, it's hard to be their fan.  "You're not my fan" implies that Ellis doesn't care for you, which is something he does care about.

text_original:
Job sectors that experienced the most gains statewide from September to October were retail trade, education, health care, professional and business services, state and local public schools, leisure and hospitality, technology, construction,.

Nonfarm payrolls increased by 9,000 in September, the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported today. However, the unemployment rate rose one-tenth of a percentage point to 6.6 percent, its highest level since December 2008.

Although a substantial increase in total employment occurred, wage growth was relatively weak. Employment gains were primarily supported by new job additions in professional and business services, health care, education, and mining and logging, which provided nearly all of the job gains in September. New job creation in these sectors was also supported by an uptick in wage growth, which climbed to 3.1 percent in September from 2.5 percent in August.

When adjusted for seasonal factors, the unemployment rate for October remained unchanged at 6.7 percent. The current level of unemployment is lower than it has been during any of the last five expansionary periods (the last four expansions were marked by higher unemployment rates than the current rate).

Job growth continued to be led by professional and business services, where new jobs increased by 5,000.

Consumers purchasing IBM PCs will soon get their computers pre-configured for free Internet access from Juno Online Services.

Juno, a provider of online services to help consumers get the most out of their PCs, announced the availability of a free Java application – Oracle Internet Explorer – that allows customers to use the Internet Explorer browser as a default browser on their IBM PC, without requiring any extra software or drivers.

"We're excited by our partnership with IBM," said Lisa LeMay, senior vice president of Oracle Internet Explorer. "We're happy that we can now offer consumers easy access to the Internet and other applications that they need without having to download drivers or mess with any software on their computer."

The Java application, which is available in English only, is included in the IBM PC Standard Edition, IBM's pre-configured IBM PC system that includes a 967MHz processor, 512MB RAM, and a 320MB hard drive.

IBM has made its PCs pre-configured with the Java application for years, but the new version is the first time that a company other than IBM has offered the service.

Photos by Brett Luke | The Journal Gazette Angelia Ware Grundy pays respect Sunday afternoon to victims of unsolved homicide cases, including her grandsons Shawn Ware and Donta Grundy.

The state police have asked for the public’s help in solving three homicides that occurred within 24 hours of one another in August 2009.

Download the mobile version of this story for free on your smart phone or tablet.

Tampa police are trying to determine whether a woman found dead in an apartment condominium was murdered or died by natural causes.

The man suspected in the murders is still at large.

Also Sunday, police arrested a suspect connected to the August 2009 slaying of two brothers from the Detroit area.

Investigators were looking for a man who they say stole a car during an attempted robbery.

It happened just before 7 a.m. Friday on the 100 block of East Boston Street in Tampa. Police say the suspect left the scene with the vehicle.

A few blocks away, a man was found dead in the alley behind the 300 block of South Central Avenue, according to the Hillsborough County Sheriff’s Office.

Authorities believe he died by suicide.

text_original:
These are turbulent times for the travel sector. But if you're a fan of the industry, and want to make sure you're getting the best deals in the market, you're going to need to keep your eyes peeled.

For every two days of travel spending the average holidaymaker spends 1.6 days away from home, according to new research.

On top of that, just 1.8 per cent of all UK holidaymakers flew outside the country this year – and not all of those were on their holidays.

The research, by website Hotels.com, also found that 55 per cent of those polled said they would be willing to pay more for their next break to be closer to where they live – with 2.2 million people saying they'd be prepared to shell out an extra £300 for a trip closer to home.

The summer festive period has been hit by an increase in domestic flights, which are expected to reach record highs this week.

And the latest figures suggest that the economy is picking up, with the UK economy growing at its fastest rate since 2009.

The summer festive period has been hit by an increase in domestic flights , which are expected to reach record highs this week . This year, just 1.8 per cent of all UK holidaymakers flew outside the country – and not all of those were on their holidays, on top of that. By website Hotels.com, the research also found that 55 per cent of those polled said they would be willing to pay more for their next break to be closer to where they live – with an extra £300 for a trip closer to home, 2.2 million people saying they’d be prepared to shell out. An increase in domestic flights, which are expected to reach record highs this week, has hit the summer festive period. With the UK economy growing at its fastest rate since 2009, the latest figures suggest that the economy is picking up.

text_original:
Whoa! Barbara is lashing out at Selena in a new interview! She’s blaming the ‘Wizards of Waverly Place’ star for all the drama that surrounds their twisted ‘love’ triangle with Justin Bieber!
Barbara. Harsh.
We did not see this one coming!
The 49-year-old actress, who plays Selena Gomez on “ Wizards of Waverly Place ” told Teen Vogue in a new interview that she and Selena are “two peas in a pod” and that her character’s struggle with depression was the inspiration behind how she deals with her own issues!
“The character has a lot of issues that were very close to my heart,” Barbara said.
The story of Gomez’s battle with depression was brought to life on-screen by the Canadian actress in an episode of the CW’s hit series.
But Barbara believes it was actually her relationship with Selena that inspired her to even consider taking on the role of the young girl with the dark secret.
“You know, I have been going through some things myself, so it’s very relatable to me. It’s kind of like, you know, I’ve been there,” she explained.
